name: civipy

metadata.name: civipy
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: CiviCRM v3/v4 REST API Python wrapper

documentation:
## Getting Started
This is a brand-new project and code is subject to change at any time.

Install with `python3 -m pip install civipy`. If you are using a Python version
older than 3.11 and want to use `pyproject.toml` configuration, install with
`python3 -m pip install civipy[pyproject]`.

## Configuration

Configure your credentials in environment variables, a `.civipy` file, or in
`pyproject.toml` in a `tools.civipy` section. By default, CiviPy will read a `.civipy`
file anywhere in the current working directory up to your project root, or your
project's `pyproject.toml` file, or a `.civipy` file in the user's home folder.
Settings in environment variables will overwrite any file settings. Alternatively,
you can call `civipy.config.SETTINGS.init()` to set the configuration values.

| Setting                         | Environment Variable | `.civipy` File             | `pyproject.toml` File          |
|---------------------------------|----------------------|----------------------------|--------------------------------|
|                                 |                      |                            | `[tool.civipy]`                |
| Connection *(required)*         | `CIVI_REST_BASE`     | `rest_base=http://civi.py` | `rest-base = "http://civi.py"` |
| API Version                     | `CIVI_API_VERSION`   | `api_version=4`            | `api-version = "4"`            |
| Access Token *(required)*       | `CIVI_USER_KEY`      | `user_key=...`             | `user-key = "..."`             |
| Site Token *(required for v3)*  | `CIVI_SITE_KEY`      | `site_key=...`             | `site-key = "..."`             |
| Log File                        | `CIVI_LOG_FILE`      | `log_file=/tmp/civipy.log` | `log-file = "/tmp/civipy.log"` |
| Log Level                       | `CIVI_LOG_LEVEL`     | `log_level=WARNING`        | `log-level = "WARNING"`        |
| Config File                     | `CIVI_CONFIG`        |                            |                                |

### Connection
The Connection setting lets you specify the URL of your REST API, or the `cv` or
`drush` or `wp-cli` executable on your file system. If "http" is found in the setting,
the system will use http calls to the REST API. If the string "drush" is found, it
will use drush. If the string "wp" is found, it will use wp-cli. And if none of these
are found, it will attempt to call the cv command line API.

### API Version
Set to "3" to use the CiviCRM v3 API, or "4" (the default) to use the CiviCRM v4 API.

### Log File
To log to a file instead of the screen, set the Log File setting to the file path
you want to log to.

### Config File
You can specify in an environment variable either a directory to find a `.civipy`
configuration file in, or a file to read as a `.civipy` configuration file.

## Usage
There is a Django-style `.objects` attribute on each record model with `filter`, `values`,
`order_by`, and `all` methods for querying; and `create`, `delete`, and `update` methods
for making changes. Model instances also have `save` and `delete` methods.

```python
from civipy import CiviContact

contact = CiviContact.objects.filter(email_primary__email="ana@ananelson.com")[0]
contact.nick_name = "Ana"
contact.save()
```

Each CiviCRM Entity is represented by a subclass of CiviCRMBase; if you need an entity
that is not in the project, you can easily add it by subclassing CiviCRMBase and naming
it the entity name prepended with "Civi", e.g.

```python
import civipy

class CiviNewEntity(civipy.CiviCRMBase):
    pass
```

methods.name: civipy.membership.CiviMembership.payments
documentation: Find all MembershipPayment records associated with this Membership.

methods.name: civipy.membership.CiviMembership.apply_contribution
documentation: Apply a Contribution to this Membership and extend the expiration date.

classes.name: civipy.user.CiviUFMatch
documentation:
This is the table that matches host system users to CiviCRM Contacts.

create requires uf_id, uf_name, and contact_id

Attributes:
    id: str e.g. "24392"
    domain_id: str e.g. "1"
    uf_id: str e.g. "46914"
    uf_name: str e.g. "user@example.com"
    contact_id: str e.g. "367872"

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.get
documentation: Make an API request with the "get" action and return the full response.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.create
documentation:
Make an API request with the "create" action and return an object of class cls
populated with the created object's data.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.update
documentation:
Update the current object with the values specified in kwargs. Returns the full
API response.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.save
documentation: Save the current instance.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.find
documentation:
Looks for an existing object in CiviCRM with parameters equal to the values
specified in kwargs. If using API v4 and select is specified, the result will include
the specified keys.

Returns an object of class cls populated with this object's data if found, otherwise
returns None.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.find_all
documentation:
Looks for multiple existing objects in CiviCRM with parameters equal to the
values specified in kwargs. If using API v4 and select is specified, the result will
include the specified keys.

Returns a list of objects of class cls populated with data. Returns an empty list
if no matching values found.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.find_and_update
documentation:
Looks for an existing object in CiviCRM with parameters equal to the values
specified in `where`.

If a unique record is found, record is also updated with values in `kwargs`.

Returns an object of class cls populated with this object's data if found, otherwise
returns None.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.find_or_create
documentation:
Looks for an existing object in CiviCRM with parameters search_keys equal to the
values for search_keys specified in kwargs.

If a unique record is found and do_update is True, record is also updated with
values in `kwargs`.

If no record is found, a new record is created with the data in `where` and `kwargs`.

Returns an object of class cls populated with the found, updated, or created
object's data.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.action
documentation: Calls the CiviCRM API action and returns parsed JSON on success.

methods.name: civipy.base.base.CiviCRMBase.pprint
documentation: Print the current record's data in a human-friendly format.

functions.name: civipy.base.config.read_dot_civipy
documentation: read settings from .civipy file

functions.name: civipy.base.config.read_pyproject_toml
documentation: read settings from pyproject.toml file

functions.name: civipy.base.config.find_config_file
documentation:
Find a .civipy or pyproject.toml file. Look in current directory up to
project root then check user's home directory for .civipy.

methods.name: civipy.base.config.Settings.init
documentation: set configuration settings then set up logging

methods.name: civipy.base.config.Settings._read_config
documentation: Read configuration from wherever found

methods.name: civipy.base.config.Settings.__getattr__
documentation: Look up config value, with fallback to read environment variables or config files

methods.name: civipy.contribution.CiviContribution.complete_transaction
documentation: Calls the CiviCRM API completetransaction action and returns parsed JSON on success.

methods.name: civipy.contribution.CiviContribution.find_by_transaction_id
documentation: Find a contribution by payment transaction ID

methods.name: civipy.contribution.CiviContribution.find_by_donor
documentation:
Find a contribution by donor's display name, and optionally
by amount and/or date received (yyyy-mm-dd).

methods.name: civipy.contribution.CiviContribution.find_by_donor_id
documentation:
Find a contribution by donor's contact ID, and optionally
by amount and/or date received (yyyy-mm-dd).

methods.name: civipy.contribution.CiviContributionRecur.find_by_transaction_id
documentation: Find a recurring contribution by subscription transaction ID

methods.name: civipy.option_group.CiviOptionGroup.find_options_by_group_name
documentation: Taking an option_group_name, looks up the group and its members.

methods.name: civipy.option_group.CiviOptionGroup.option_values_dict_by_group_name
documentation: Taking an option_group_name, looks up the group and its members.

methods.name: civipy.financial.CiviEntityFinancialTrxn.find_by_transaction_id
documentation: Find a Contribution Payment by payment transaction ID

methods.name: civipy.order.CiviPayment.find_by_transaction_id
documentation: Find a Contribution Payment by payment transaction ID

classes.name: civipy.interface.base.BaseInterface
documentation:
Interface manages communication with the CiviCRM API

Call the Interface instance with `action`, `entity`, and `params` values to submit an API request.

The `search_query` method is a helper function to generate query parameters.
The `values` method is a helper function to generate parameters for create/update.

methods.name: civipy.interface.query.BaseQuery.__getitem__
documentation: Retrieve an item or slice from the set of results.

methods.name: civipy.interface.query.BaseQuery._interface
documentation:
Instantiate the appropriate API interface and store it on the base Query class,
so that it will be instantiated only once and available to all entity classes.

methods.name: civipy.interface.query.BaseQuery._compose
documentation: Create a query for the selected API version from the current attributes.

methods.name: civipy.interface.query.BaseQuery._deep_update
documentation: Update `query` with the values in `other`, also updating contained dicts rather than overwriting them.

methods.name: civipy.address.CiviAddress.create
documentation:
Calls the CiviCRM API create action and returns parsed JSON on success.

Accepts field country_iso_code and will convert this to country_id
Accepts field state_province_abbreviation and will convert this to state_province_id

classes.name: civipy.mailing.CiviMailingEventQueue
documentation:
This is the table that links CiviMail mailings to Contacts.

Attributes:
    id: str e.g. "1202"
    job_id: str e.g. "27"
    email_id: str e.g. "96173"
    hash: str e.g. "1fc2d50bfafd5c27"
    contact_id: str e.g. "16257"

methods.name: civipy.contact.CiviGroup.find_by_title
documentation: Finds a CiviGroup object for the group entitled "title".
